channel.title: Morning Transportation
channel.description: Delivered every Monday by 10 a.m., Weekly Transportation examines the latest news in transportation and infrastructure politics and policy.

item.title: Going deeper on DOT spending bill
item.description: We take a closer look at the House GOP’s Transportation-HUD spending bill, which has many noteworthy restrictions on how the money could be spent.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Ogozalek and Oriana Pawlyk
item.dc:contributor: By Sam Ogozalek and Oriana Pawlyk

item.title: Surface bill text drops
item.description: The House Transportation Committee plans to hold its surface reauthorization markup on Thursday. And get ready for lots of appropriations action.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Ogozalek and Chris Marquette
item.dc:contributor: By Sam Ogozalek and Chris Marquette

item.title: Who paid for Sean Duffy’s road trip videos
item.description: The production costs for a video series about DOT chief Sean Duffy and his family driving across the country weren’t paid for by taxpayers, an agency spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Ogozalek

item.title: After Spirit’s demise, federal aid for other budget airlines unlikely
item.description: Spirit Airlines collapsed. It’s an uphill battle for other low-cost carriers in their bid for federal aid.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Ogozalek

item.title: The surface markup waiting game
item.description: The House Transportation Committee won’t consider the next surface reauthorization this week. When will it mark up the bill?
item.dc:creator: By Sam Ogozalek and Chris Marquette
item.dc:contributor: By Sam Ogozalek and Chris Marquette

item.title: The reconciliation question
item.description: Will DOT chief Sean Duffy get a chance to snag more air traffic control funding in a new reconciliation bill?
item.dc:creator: By Sam Ogozalek and Chris Marquette
item.dc:contributor: By Sam Ogozalek and Chris Marquette

item.title: A busy transportation week ahead
item.description: Congress’ lengthy recess is over, with several transportation items on the agenda this week, including a vote on the ALERT Act.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Ogozalek and Oriana Pawlyk
item.dc:contributor: By Sam Ogozalek and Oriana Pawlyk

item.title: Budget szn
item.description: Here’s everything you need to know about how much money the White House wants for DOT in fiscal 2027 — and what it aims to do with it.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Ogozalek
